metadata.title: States PCS: Bihar GK for BPSC and other Bihar PCS Exams

questions.question: Who founded the Bihar School of Yoga (Munger) ?
questions.explanation: Bihar School of Yoga was founded by Swami Satyanad Saraswati. It was established in 1963 in Munger. An institute of Yogic studies was created in 1994. The school teaches traditional yoga in a modern style. A magazine “Yoga Magazine” is published by Bihar School of Yoga. It has now become an international organization with more than 50 ashrams worldwide.

questions.question: In which year, Bihar Education Project Council was formed?
questions.explanation: Bihar Education Project Council was formed in 1991. The main aim of the formation of council was bringing about quantitative and qualitative improvement in the Elementary Education system in Bihar. The council is responsible for making elementary education stronger by improving education standard in elementary schools. It also encourages school age children to join school and prevent dropouts from school. The main office of Bihar Education Project Council is at Patna.

questions.question: Who built Rohtas Fort in Bihar?
questions.explanation: Rohtasgarh Fort in Bihar was built by Sher Shah Suri, founder of the Suri Empire, as a stronghold against Mughal advances. It is a notable example of Muslim military architecture in India.

questions.question: Bangar or Bhangar is the old alluvial soil. In which region of Bihar, the extent of Bangar soil is maximum?
questions.explanation: The maximum extent of Bhangar soil is found in Purnia and Saharsa district. Bhangar soil is limeless and Alkali free. Its color is thick. The soil is less fertile as compared to Khadar soil. Old Alluvial soil is called Bhangar.

questions.question: Which of the following river is the longest northern tributary of Ganga in Bihar?
questions.explanation: Kosi river is the longest northern tributary of Ganga. It is also known as Sapta Kosi means seven rivers. It is known as the “Sorrow of Bihar” as the annual floods affect about 21,000 km sq (8,100 sq mi) of fertile agricultural lands thereby disturbing the rural economy.

questions.question: Which of the following sanctuaries have been covered under the ‘Project Tiger’ in Bihar?
questions.explanation: Valmiki National Park has been covered under the ‘Project Tiger’ in Bihar. Valmiki National Park is a Tiger Reserve in the West Champaran District of Bihar. It is the only national park in Bihar. Valmiki Tiger Reserve covers 898.45 km² which is 17.4% of the total geographical area of the district. As per the data of 2018, there were 40 tigers in the Reserve.

questions.question: Which organization has maximum number of roads under their control in Bihar?
questions.explanation: In Bihar, Zila Parishad has maximum number of roads under their control.

questions.question: In which city of Bihar, land has been allotted to open a branch of Aligarh Muslim University?
questions.explanation: Land has been allotted at Kishanganj for establishing the branch of Aligarh Muslim University.

questions.question: For what purpose, Golghar was built in Patna?
questions.explanation: Golghar was built in Patna for the Storage of grains. It was built in 1786 by the Architect Captain John Garstin. The architectural style is same as Stupa. It is 96 feet high and its lower wall is 12 feet thick.

questions.question: Tilak Swarajya Fund was started to finance the Non-Cooperation Movement. How much money was collected in Bihar for the Non-Cooperation Movement?
questions.explanation: An amount of 7.5 lakh rupees was collected in Bihar for the Non-Cooperation Movement. Non-cooperation movement was launched on 4 September 1920 by Mahatma Gandhi with the aim of self-governance and obtaining full independence.
